title: senna ferraria
intro: Senna ferraria, also known as the fern-leaved senna, is a perennial herb native to South Africa, belonging to the legume family. The plant contains anthraquinone glycosides, which have been used in traditional medicine for their purgative and laxative properties. The medicinal properties of Senna ferraria have been traditionally used to treat constipation, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues, offering relief from symptoms such as bloating and indigestion. Herbal preparations of Senna ferraria can be found in tea, capsule, or powder forms, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their effects.
